arguments.description: Number of threads to perform bruteforcing. Default = 1

arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the frameworks category. Fits when the engagement needs: kerbrute An script to perform kerberos bruteforcing by using the Impacket library.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: kerbrute An script to perform kerberos bruteforcing by using the Impacket library.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: pip3 install kerbrute; git clone https://github.com/TarlogicSecurity/kerbrute. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `Impacket v0.9.18 - Copyright 2018 SecureAuth Corporation`. Further invocations from upstream: `usage: kerbrute.py [-h] [-debug] (-user USER | -users USERS)`; `./kerbrute.py -users users_file.txt -passwords passwords_file.txt -domain contoso.com`. kerbrute An script to perform kerberos bruteforcing by using the Impacket library.

name: kerbrute
summary: kerbrute An script to perform kerberos bruteforcing by using the Impacket library.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need frameworks, use kerbrute because kerbrute An script to perform kerberos bruteforcing by using the Impacket library.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Impacket v0.9.18 - Copyright 2018 SecureAuth Corporation` as documented upstream for kerbrute.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `usage: kerbrute.py [-h] [-debug] (-user USER | -users USERS)` as documented upstream for kerbrute.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./kerbrute.py -users users_file.txt -passwords passwords_file.txt -domain contoso.com` as documented upstream for kerbrute.

When you need a concrete invocation, run `root@kali:~# kerbrute -domain jurassic.park -users users.txt -passwords passwords.txt -outputfile jurassic_passwords.txt` as documented upstream for kerbrute.
When chaining frameworks tools, pair kerbrute with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `kerbrute --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
